sector_rotation.explanation: No clear rotation signal. Cyclical and defensive sectors are performing similarly — market may be in a transitional phase.

news_headlines.title: USA Rare Earth to Acquire Serra Verde in $2.8 Billion Deal - WSJ
Mercedes C-Class Electric Revealed: 'As Smooth As An S-Class' - Motor1.com
news_headlines.description: Mercedes debuts its first-ever C-Class EV. With a retro grille and an all-screen dashboard, it looks significantly different than the combustion-engine model.

news_headlines.title: Why a $33 billion stock market buying spree is now winding down - MarketWatch
news_headlines.description: The recent rally was ‘a bit much,’ says trading desk

news_headlines.title: Wheels Up Wants High-End Travelers to See Flying Private as ‘Not So Far Out of Reach’ - WSJ
news_headlines.description: The private jet company aims to make the ultimate splurge more accessible, with help from its Delta connection

news_headlines.title: PTO, parental leave, pensions: Even the most prized benefits are on the chopping block - Business Insider
news_headlines.description: In a tough labor market, workers often have fewer options for job-hopping, and employers can rethink what benefits they offer.

news_headlines.title: AI chatbots are giving people alternatives to chemotherapy, study finds - NBC News
news_headlines.description: Artificial intelligence chatbots will tell you where to find alternatives to chemotherapy if you ask them, a new study finds

news_headlines.title: Data centers are expensive, unpopular — and could be a tipping point in the midterms - NPR
news_headlines.description: Anger over the data center boom has spilled into politics with voters unseating local politicians who support them. It's become an issue hard to ignore in the midterm elections.

news_headlines.title: How the Iran war set Beijing up for global clean energy dominance - Politico
news_headlines.description: Countries are navigating between the desire to speed up the green transition and worries over Beijing’s clean-tech dominance.

news_headlines.title: Dow futures fall over 350 points as Iranian war tensions escalate: Live updates - CNBC
news_headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Sunday said the U.S. had fired on and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman.

news_headlines.title: The narrow foundations of the current rally — one company is responsible for half of S&P 500 earnings revisions - MarketWatch
Rising Real Estate Trend: 'Accidental Landlords' - Newser
news_headlines.description: Homeowners struggling to sell are turning to rental income instead

news_headlines.title: Wall Street Journal Sees Unwelcome Era for Workers - Newser
news_headlines.description: 'Welcome to the age of the mega-layoff,' the site declares in an analysis

news_headlines.title: Director of the CBO—known for gloomy national debt data—is optimistic that a crisis will be avoided - Fortune
news_headlines.description: Phill Swagel, director of the Congressional Budget Office, is optimistic that a national debt crisis will be avoided.

news_headlines.title: Scoop: NSA using Anthropic's Mythos despite Defense Department blacklist - Axios
news_headlines.description: The government's cybersecurity needs are outweighing the Pentagon's feud with Anthropic.

news_headlines.title: Bitcoin, ether, solana slide, oil jumps on renewed U.S.-Iran war risks - CoinDesk
news_headlines.description: Bitcoin traded at $74,335 after Iran reimposed controls on the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, with the modest 1.6% pullback standing out against a 5.7% jump in Brent and a 1.2% drop in European equity futures.

nyt_headlines.title: What to Expect in Apple’s New Era
nyt_headlines.abstract: Tim Cook’s replacement as chief executive, John Ternus, knows hardware engineering. Can he be a product visionary and a global diplomat?

nyt_headlines.title: UnitedHealth Group Reports Flat First-Quarter Earnings
nyt_headlines.abstract: One of the nation’s largest insurers, the company’s profits were higher than analysts’ expectations but still fell short of showing a significant comeback.

nyt_headlines.title: How Apple Became a $4 Trillion Company Under Tim Cook
nyt_headlines.abstract: If the Steve Jobs era was defined by technological innovation, the Tim Cook period was one of exceptional financial growth.

nyt_headlines.title: Trump’s Pick to Lead Fed Will Face Senators at a Tricky Moment
nyt_headlines.abstract: Kevin M. Warsh has enough support to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve, but a criminal investigation into the central bank stands in his way.

nyt_headlines.title: Supreme Court Reviews F.C.C.’s Enforcement Power Against Communications Companies
nyt_headlines.abstract: AT&T and Verizon were penalized millions of dollars for what the agency said was a failure to protect consumer information. The companies say they were deprived of their right to a jury trial.

nyt_headlines.title: Baggage Fees Are Up. Here’s How to Avoid Them.
nyt_headlines.abstract: Airline passengers are feeling the pain in rising fees, as the war in Iran weighs on the price of jet fuel. Loyalty programs and certain credit cards can help ease the burden.

nyt_headlines.title: ‘Wagyu’ Used to Guarantee Quality Beef. What Are You Paying for Today?
nyt_headlines.abstract: Behind the scenes, competing forces battle for the reputation of prestige meat.

nyt_headlines.title: Even Without Internet Access, Prisoners Are Trying to Benefit From A.I.
nyt_headlines.abstract: Most prisons forbid online access. But inmates are finding ways to ask chatbots questions anyway.

nyt_headlines.title: Oil Prices Recede and Stocks Gain as Investors Eye Peace Talks
nyt_headlines.abstract: Oil prices pulled back and stocks inched higher as investors looked to the possibility of a second round of peace talks between the United States and Iran.

nyt_headlines.title: Second Jury Finds Uber Responsible for Sexual Assault by a Driver
nyt_headlines.abstract: The ride-hailing giant has now lost the first two of more than 3,000 pending federal lawsuits.

nyt_headlines.title: The Onion Has a New Plan to Take Over Infowars
nyt_headlines.abstract: A new deal, which would allow The Onion to use the Infowars name and website address, must be approved by a Texas judge.

nyt_headlines.title: Trump’s Fed Pick to Defend Central Bank’s Independence on Rate Setting
nyt_headlines.abstract: Kevin M. Warsh is set to testify at the Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday even as a criminal investigation into the central bank risks delaying his ascension to become the next chair.

nyt_headlines.title: The Great Hesitation
nyt_headlines.abstract: Nonstop political chaos is badly damaging our economy.

nyt_headlines.title: The Peace Rally Gets Derailed
nyt_headlines.abstract: Oil prices are climbing and global stocks are falling as investors worry about the prospects for a speedy end to the war in the Middle East.

nyt_headlines.title: Hey, Washington: Guess Who’s Coming to Dinner
nyt_headlines.abstract: A president who relishes attacking the news media is set to break his boycott of an event celebrating the news media. (The first lady is attending, too.) What could go wrong?
